message:
fix: prevent HardwareHealthCard from overriding user's explicit view choice (#3798) (#3811)

The auto-switch useEffect unconditionally set viewMode to 'alerts' whenever
activeAlertCount > 0, including on background data refreshes. This forced
users back to the Alerts tab even after they explicitly switched to Inventory.

Add a userSelectedView ref that is set when the user clicks any view tab.
The auto-switch logic now only fires on initial load (before any user
interaction), preserving the user's explicit choice on subsequent refreshes.

Co-authored-by: andy anderson <andy@andys-MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
